name: Stats Section
description: Marketing-oriented statistics section with large headline metrics, supporting labels, and optional narrative framing.

hero.description: Large featured metrics row for landing pages, with one short section intro above.

slots.intro: Headline and supporting copy for the metrics section
slots.stat-grid: Responsive grid of large stat cards or tiles
slots.stat-item: Value, label, and short supporting qualifier

compact.description: Denser summary row for product proof or pricing sections.

visual_brief: Confident marketing stats section with oversized values, restrained labels, and enough spacing to let each number breathe. The values should feel like proof points in a narrative rather than dashboard telemetry. Supporting copy stays concise and compositional.

responsive.mobile: Single-column or two-column stack with reduced headline scale so values remain readable without crowding.
responsive.tablet: Two to four metrics per row depending on count, with clear separation between intro copy and the metrics themselves.
responsive.desktop: Wide balanced stat grid with one intro band above and evenly weighted proof points below.

layout_hints.narrative_role: This pattern is for public proof and trust, not operations dashboards. Values should lead, labels should support, and qualifiers should stay brief.
layout_hints.grid_balance: All stat tiles should feel rhythmically aligned even when one value is much longer than the others.
layout_hints.surface_choice: Cards are optional. The pattern may use simple tiles or low-chrome blocks depending on theme, but the values should always dominate the visual hierarchy.

motion.micro: Counters may animate in gently on reveal, and dividers or accents may fade into place.
motion.transitions: Entry should feel polished and confidence-building, not playful or hyperactive.

keyboard: If stat items are interactive, keep tab order linear and expose one clear target per tile.
announcements: Metric labels and values should be announced together so the meaning of each number is preserved.

composition.StatsSection: Stack(d-section, proof) > [Intro?(d-label + heading + body) + StatGrid(d-data, responsive) > StatItem[]]
